dialogos.texto_en: (While being questioned by the four sisters,\n we made our way to Ocean Expo Park%p-2;――%p;)
(Immediately after entering Churaumi Aquarium,\n we came upon a large tank at the front.)
Waahh...!
Amaaazing...
Yes, it's breathtaking...
Oh, a whale shark is coming this way.
Wow, so impactful!
At this size, it's not just a tank, it's like the ocean!
It certainly is big, but calling it an ocean is \na little bit of an overstatement.
I've heard about it,\nbut it's hard to believe there's a tank this big.
It's really surprising.\nI've seen photos, but it's beyond my imagination.
Ah, just seeing this\nmakes me feel like it was worth coming.
I wonder how many fish are in here?
Alrighty, let's count them!
Um, isn't that a bit reckless?
Even just looking at this tank\ncould take up all our time,\nbut there are so many other things to see.
Yes, and as for the attractions,\nwe can view this large tank from above.
From above? That sounds interesting.
I heard there's also\nan exhibit of deep-sea fish over there.
I want to take a closer look\nat the tropical fish tank we saw earlier.
Oh dear, it seems like\nwe all want to see different things again.
Then let's just wander around the aquarium on our own.
Alright.\nThen let's meet up here afterwards.
Okaaay!
What about you, Futaro-kun?\nIs there anywhere you want to go?
Honestly, I'm still feeling tired from this morning.\nI don't really feel like moving around much, so I was\nthinking of chilling here and watching the large tank.
Well, in that case,\nI researched a special spot beforehand.\nWhy don't we go there?
A special spot?
Hooray, we got such a great spot to sit at.\nWe hit the jackpot, didn't we?
Right in front of the large tank.\nIt really feels like we're underwater here.\nI can understand why you call it a special spot.
I know, riiight? I thought it would be amazing\nto sit here when I was doing my research.\nWe're really lucky to have found it.
Well, let's order some food for now.
It feels like...I could lose track of time here.
Yeah, I could watch this foreeever.
Oh? What kind of fish\nwas that school that just swam by?
That was probably the giant trevally,\npopular among anglers.
Oh, it's a type of jackfish.\nIt looks so different from the ones you see in the markets.\nBut maybe it's delicious, since it's a jackfish?
I wonder.\nI've never tasted it before.
Well, even if you had,\nyour assessment of the flavor would be questionable.
Leave me alone.
Do you know any other types of fish?
Well, there are a few, but...\nI don't know most kinds, to be honest.
Wow, even you don't know some of them, Futaro-kun?\nIs it because there are so many different types?
No, I just don't really know much about fish species.\nIt's not like it's going to be on a test or anything.
Haha, yeah, I guess you're right.\nNino and Raiha-chan\nprobably know more about fish than we do.
So, what other kinds of fish%p-2;――%p; wow!
You're a bit too surprised,\njust because a manta ray swam by.
Of course I'd be surprised!\nIt came out of nowhere!
If you look closely at this tank,\nthere are a few creatures that look like manta rays,\nbut they all seem slightly different.
Hmm? Now that you mention it, you're right.\nLet me look it up on my phone real quick.
Let's see, apparently, mantas swim near the surface of\nthe ocean and eat plankton. On the other hand,\nstingrays eat things like clams near the ocean floor.
Oh, you can tell them apart just by their appearance.\nLook, a picture comparison came up.
Hmm? Let me see, now?
Oh, I see.\nTheir facial features are different\nbecause they have different diets.
I-I see.
Huh?\nWhat's wrong%p-2;――
S-sorry! You got too close!
No, it's okay.
...I was just thinking.\nThis sort of thing...\nIt's like we're together, you know?
W-well, I guess.\nIt just feels like\nI can't get used to it...or something...
I have a feeling I'll never really get used to this.\nMy feelings for you, I mean...
Gosh, look at me.\nSorry, I didn't mean to get all embarrassing like that.
Alrighty! Let's have a contest to see\nwho can tell the difference between\nmanta rays and stingrays better!
Y-yeah! Bring it on!
That's all for your order.\nPlease take your time and enjoy.
Thaaank you very much.
Ichika, is that latte art or somethin' on your drink?
I'm not sure what kinda fish,\nbut there are so many cute little fishies here.\nIt's so nice to receive this kind of hospitality.
Oh, I'll take a picture.
And, upload it...
Upload?
Upload it onto social media.\nHave you never used it before, Futaro-kun?
I don't know how to use it.
Whaaat?\nIt's convenient for us to keep in touch, \nso hurry up and learn it already.
I just find it bothersome.\nBesides, if amateurs meddle with it,\nthey might fall for scams, right?
Pff! Ahaha!\nAre you serious, Futaro-kun?
Obviously?
Oh, I see.\nYou're sometimes overly cautious,\neven though you can be incredibly bold too.
If you're careful, you'll be safe from scams.\nSo why not give it a try? No pressure.
You think so?
(Maybe I should give social media a shot.)
Can't we just text?\nThat should be enough for communication.
That's so like you, Futaro-kun.\nOf course, we can use email to stay in touch, but%p-2;――
Social media is easier,\nso you might feel like contacting me\njust for trivial things.
I'd be happy if I could have a casual chat with you,\neven when we're far apart.
If you put it that way... Sounds good.
(Maybe I should give social media a shot.)
Well, that's the plan.\nIt seems convenient in some way.
Oh my, impressive, impressive.\nIt makes me happy\nto see you taking initiative, Futaro-kun.
There's nothing wrong\nwith making it easier to stay in touch.
Then, when you start using it,\nlet's create a group chat just for the two of us.
A group ch—? What?
I'll explain all of that to you,\nstep by step.
Okay, that's a relief.
(Maybe I should give social media a shot.)
...Phew.
(Giving more effort just to communicate \n with other people, huh?\n I never thought I'd see the day%p-2;――%p;)
(I've changed a lot, surprisingly.\n But this is good. No, this is great.)
All right, I'll take some photos too!\nThey'll come in handy when I start social media!
Oh? You're motivated.\nI'm looking forward to the messages you'll send me.
